x i sh: noun: A mid-frequency radio transmission or broadcast signal.
q' r i: noun: An active, autonomous Martas defense drone.
a r t' a: noun: The root execution language and underlying logic of the enclave.
k' a x: verb: To physically strip or scavenge a hardware component from a larger system.
c a c: verb: To physically splice or bridge two live electrical wires.
f i r: verb: To execute a continuous software process or loop.
h u sh: verb: To place a system into a low-voltage idle or standby state.
gh a sh: verb: To aggressively overclock a hardware component past its stable baseline.
s i r: noun: A physical copper wire or data bus.
t' u c: noun: A physical, hand-held maintenance tool.
c i t': noun: An environmental or industrial hardware sensor (e.g., accelerometer or gyro).
f u x: noun: Liquid coolant or industrial fluid used in thermal management.
q' a: noun: A physically insulated, offline sanctuary safe from network intrusion.
c i r: verb: To fetch or read raw data from a specific memory address.
k' i x: verb: To permanently flash or burn data onto a hardened ROM chip.
h i sh: verb: To echo or output a raw signal through a hardware terminal.
q' u t': verb: To logically partition or reserve a sector of raw storage.
f a c: verb: To securely flush a buffer or drop a software connection.
t' i k': noun: A logical pointer directing execution to a specific hardware address.
s u ng: noun: A temporary data buffer or holding queue.
x a: noun: An empty, unwritten sector of memory; a null pointer.
prefix: [FIRST PERSON] Binds the opcode execution to the speaker (I/Me).
prefix: [FIRST PLURAL] Binds the opcode execution to the speaker's group (We).
prefix: [SECOND PERSON] Binds the opcode execution to the target entity (You).
modifier: [QUEUED] The High-State dot. Indicates an action scheduled to happen (Future).
modifier: [ACTIVE] The Neutral-State dot. Indicates an action currently executing (Present).
modifier: [LOGGED] The Low-State dot. Indicates an action that has concluded and written to memory (Past).
modifier: [GATE] The Relay dot. Evaluates the following phrase as a condition (IF).
modifier: [NOT] The Friction dot. Inverts or nullifies the following opcode or register (NO/FALSE).
modifier: [VECTOR IN] The Vent dot. Indicates movement into or location inside a structure or port.
modifier: [VECTOR OUT] The Exhaust dot. Indicates movement out of or location external to a structure.
modifier: [VECTOR HIGH] The Static dot. Indicates location above, on top of, or at a higher physical elevation.
modifier: [VECTOR LOW] The Drone dot. Indicates location below, underneath, or grounded beneath an object.
modifier: [VECTOR PIN] The Switch dot. Indicates an exact, physical connection or adjacent location (at/on).
prefix: [EXTERNAL POINTER] Binds the opcode execution to a third-party or unidentified entity (He/She/It/They).
modifier: [QUERY FLAG] The Breaker dot. Casts the following phrase as a polling request for data (Question).
modifier: [AND GATE] The Cracking Relay dot. Binds two conditions, registers, or opcodes together (Boolean AND).
modifier: [OR GATE] The Motor Trill dot. Offers a diverging branch between two choices (Boolean OR).
modifier: [EXECUTION TRIGGER] The Hardware Bus. Acts as a 'THEN' gate, bridging a logical condition to its resulting execution.
